name: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Management of Connections
description: A J2EE application directly manages database connections using DriverManager.getConnection() instead of using the container-provided connection pool via DataSource. This bypasses container resource management, leading to connection leaks, poor scalability, and quality degradation.
detection_pattern: Look for STRUCTURAL nodes (J2EE application code) making EXTERNAL calls (DriverManager.getConnection(), direct JDBC connections) to STORAGE nodes (database) without using the proper AUTH/STRUCTURAL mediator (container DataSource, JNDI lookup, connection pool) that provides managed connection lifecycle.
